_comment: Single source of truth for every non-nixpkgs macOS app installed on the NC production Macs. Consumed by overlays/production-apps.nix (builtins.fromJSON) and kept up to date by scripts/update_packages.py. Do not hand-edit version/url/sha256 for 'auto' packages -- run the script instead.

packages.name: spotify
packages.note: manual-local, kindDetail external: never committed to git at all (see vendor/README.md) -- placed by hand at this fixed absolute path on each Mac during initial provisioning, e.g. via `python3 scripts/update_packages.py provision --staging <dir>`. Originally pinned because Spotify's real download (download.scdn.co) is an unversioned rolling build with no vendor-published hash -- this way it's always the same known-good build instead of whatever's currently live. Does NOT need pkg-extraction like the Blackmagic/Audinate entries -- the dmg already contains Spotify.app directly. After placing the file, run: python3 scripts/update_packages.py pkg update spotify <version> -- that computes localSha256 for you.

packages.name: dante-controller
packages.note: Upstream ships this as a .dmg containing DanteController.pkg, not a bare .pkg -- see kind override handling in lib/mac-apps.nix (dmgContainsPkg).

packages.name: blackmagic-desktop-video
packages.note: manual-local, kindDetail externalDmgContainsPkg: this installer is ~700MB+ -- nowhere near GitHub's 100MB push limit even with Git LFS's free tier -- so it's never committed at all (see vendor/README.md), just placed by hand at this fixed absolute path on each Mac. Confirmed this ships as a plain .dmg (not zip-wrapped) containing the real installer .pkg -- no manual extraction needed, Nix pulls the .pkg out of the .dmg itself at build time (lib/mac-apps.nix's mkPkgFromLocalDmg). Just place the raw .dmg here (rename to match this exact filename), e.g. via `python3 scripts/update_packages.py provision --staging <dir>`, then run: python3 scripts/update_packages.py pkg update blackmagic-desktop-video <version>.

packages.name: atem-software-control
packages.note: manual-local, kindDetail externalDmgContainsPkg: same situation as blackmagic-desktop-video, if anything worse -- the 'Blackmagic ATEM Switchers' installer download runs multiple GB. Confirmed this is also a plain .dmg wrapping the real installer .pkg -- no manual extraction needed, Nix pulls the .pkg out at build time. Never committed to git; place the raw .dmg here (rename to match this exact filename), then run: python3 scripts/update_packages.py pkg update atem-software-control <version>.

packages.name: dante-virtual-soundcard
packages.note: manual-local, kindDetail external: Dante Virtual Soundcard requires a purchased license tied to an Audinate account, so there is no public direct-download URL -- never committed to git (see vendor/README.md), placed by hand at this fixed path. Extract the .pkg out of the .dmg Audinate ships, place it here, then run: python3 scripts/update_packages.py pkg update dante-virtual-soundcard <version>.

packages.name: klang-app
packages.note: Corrected from an earlier 'klang-fabrik' placeholder: the actual product for in-ear monitoring control is KLANG:app (not Klang:fabrik), and unlike grandMA3/Blackmagic/DVS it turns out to have a plain, stable wp-content download URL -- no account/token gate -- so this is a normal fetchurl-based entry, not a manual/local one. It's also wrapped two layers deep (.zip > .dmg > .app), handled by kindDetail zipContainsDmg / lib/mac-apps.nix's mkAppFromZippedDmg. appName is my best guess at the exact bundle name from KLANG's own branding -- if the real folder name inside the dmg differs, the build fails loudly with an `ls -la` of what it actually found, so it's self-correcting on the first `darwin-rebuild build`.

packages.name: grandma3-onpc
packages.note: manual-local, kindDetail external: MA Lighting gates grandMA3 onPC downloads behind an access-token URL tied to your MA account session (would break on re-fetch), and at ~700MB it's also well over GitHub's 100MB push limit anyway -- never committed to git (see vendor/README.md), placed by hand at this fixed absolute path on each Mac instead, e.g. via `python3 scripts/update_packages.py provision --staging <dir>`. pkgId above is a guess -- after the first manual install, run `pkgutil --pkgs | grep -i grandma` (or `malighting`) on the Mac and fix pkgId here if it doesn't match, otherwise modules/mac-app-activation.nix's install-once check never considers it already installed.

packages.name: desktoppr
packages.note: CLI tool that sets the desktop picture reliably from a LaunchAgent (osascript/System Events routinely no-ops when run from a non-interactive activation script). Installs to /usr/local/bin/desktoppr. Used by modules/wallpaper.nix.
